libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property is required. Prefer direct in-process tests for client methods, XML/property/header/report classes, and use small in-test servlet request/response stubs for servlet-facing classes rather than starting a WebDAV server.

deterministic_setup.reason: jackrabbit-webdav 2.0-beta4 exposes servlet-based public APIs such as WebdavRequestImpl, WebdavResponseImpl, DavServletRequest, DavServletResponse, and AbstractWebdavServlet, while the artifact POM marks servlet-api as provided so it is not supplied by the normal test scaffold dependency.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The issue is a native-image runtime failure in existing tests, so this deterministic setup only preserves compile and coverage prerequisites; metadata or test fixes may still be required.
Use the legacy javax.servlet API, not jakarta.servlet, because this pre-Jakarta artifact depends on javax.servlet types.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The artifact is a WebDAV client/server utility library with no external service requirement, but meaningful servlet-facing coverage needs the legacy Servlet API because jackrabbit-webdav declares javax.servlet:servlet-api as a provided dependency.
